name: Anna Search
description: Search the web through pluggable search providers. The initial provider is ddgs.
_comment: Publish metadata (slug/name/version/executa_type/description) is read by `anna-app apps push` / `anna-app executa publish`; the dev fields (tool_id/type/command/enabled) are read by `anna-app dev`. The `distribution` block uses the switchable multi-profile form: `profiles` declares `local` and `binary` side by side, and `active` selects which one the publish flow forwards to the server. `active: "binary"` (the production default) ships the self-contained PyInstaller binary per platform via the direct-upload PUSH model: `binary_artifacts` point at LOCAL archives under bundle/ (built by build_binary.sh — `<binary-name>-v<version>-<platform>.<ext>`, tar.gz on POSIX / zip on Windows; the packaged binary is named `anna-search-executa`, which is also the archive prefix and the entrypoint), and `anna-app apps cut` auto-uploads them straight into Anna storage (content-addressed by sha256, binary_sha256 pinned). No GitHub Release, no public URL. The `{version}` placeholder in each path is expanded at upload time, so `version` here MUST equal manifest.json `version` (the value build_binary.sh embeds in the archive name). Bump both together. For local dev, DON'T flip `active` on disk — run `anna-app apps push --profile local` to select the `local` profile for that push only (writes a `~/.anna/executa/bin/<minted-tool_id>` shim so 'Rediscover Local' turns the Executa Running). This dir is the `anna-search` handle referenced by app.json#bundled_executas, so manifest.json references it as `bundled:anna-search` and the publish flow substitutes the server-minted tool_id.
